categories.label: Authentication
categories.description: Login, logout, session management, invite tokens

categories.label: Dashboard UI
categories.description: Navigation, layout, login page, user display

categories.label: Test Rounds
categories.description: Creating, listing, updating, and completing rounds

categories.label: Support Widget
categories.description: Widget loading, form submission, context capture, variants

categories.label: API Endpoints
categories.description: Config, issues, commit, users API routes

categories.label: CLI Commands
categories.description: Init, serve, invite, revoke, users commands

testCases.title: Login with valid invite token
testCases.instructions: Open http://localhost:4747. Paste the invite token into the login field and click Sign In.
testCases.expectedResult: Login succeeds, dashboard loads with your name in the nav bar, session cookie is set.

testCases.title: Login rejects invalid token
testCases.instructions: Open http://localhost:4747. Enter 'bad-token-12345' and click Sign In.
testCases.expectedResult: Error message appears below the input field. No session cookie set.

testCases.title: Login rejects revoked user token
testCases.instructions: Use CLI to invite a user, revoke them, then try logging in with their token.
testCases.expectedResult: 403 error shown. User cannot access the dashboard.

testCases.title: Logout clears session
testCases.instructions: While logged in, click the Logout button in the top-right nav.
testCases.expectedResult: Redirected to login page. Refreshing the page still shows login. Session cookie cleared.

testCases.title: Protected routes require authentication
testCases.instructions: Clear cookies, then directly visit http://localhost:4747/api/config in the browser.
testCases.expectedResult: Returns 401 JSON: {success: false, error: 'Authentication required'}.

testCases.title: Token input field masks the token
testCases.instructions: Open login page and type into the token field. Inspect the input element.
testCases.expectedResult: Input is type=password. Characters are masked (dots/bullets), not visible as plaintext.

testCases.title: Dashboard loads when authenticated
testCases.instructions: Login with valid token. Wait for the page to fully load.
testCases.expectedResult: Nav bar shows 'Punchlist QA' title, 'Testing' and 'History' links, your name, and Logout button.

testCases.title: Navigation between Testing and History pages
testCases.instructions: Click 'History' in the nav bar, then click 'Testing' to go back.
testCases.expectedResult: History page loads at /history. Testing page loads at /. Active nav link is highlighted in blue.

testCases.title: SPA fallback serves index.html for unknown routes
testCases.instructions: While logged in, navigate to http://localhost:4747/nonexistent-page.
testCases.expectedResult: Dashboard loads (not a 404 page). React Router redirects to the Testing page.

testCases.title: Support widget visible on dashboard
testCases.instructions: Login to the dashboard. Look at the bottom-right corner of the page.
testCases.expectedResult: Purple support widget FAB button is visible. Clicking it opens the support form.

testCases.title: User name displayed in header
testCases.instructions: Login and check the right side of the nav bar.
testCases.expectedResult: Your display name (from the invite) is shown next to the Logout button.

testCases.title: Tester name displayed on completed test cards
testCases.instructions: Submit a test result (pass, fail, skip, or blocked). Look at the test card status area.
testCases.expectedResult: Text 'tested by [Your Name]' appears on the card after the status badge.

testCases.title: Issue link shown on TestCard after failure
testCases.instructions: Click Fail on a test card. In the Failure Dialog, check 'Create GitHub issue' and submit. Look at the test card.
testCases.expectedResult: A clickable issue number link (e.g. #42) appears on the card, opening the GitHub issue in a new tab.

testCases.title: Admin-only Users page
testCases.instructions: Login as an admin user. Check the nav bar for a 'Users' link. Click it. Then login as a tester and check again.
testCases.expectedResult: Admin sees 'Users' nav link and can access /users. Tester does not see the link and sees 'Admin access required' if navigating directly.

testCases.title: Invite user via Users page
testCases.instructions: As admin, go to Users page. Fill in email, name, select role, and click Invite.
testCases.expectedResult: Invite URL appears in a green box. New user appears in the table below with 'active' status.

testCases.title: Revoke user via Users page
testCases.instructions: As admin, go to Users page. Click 'Revoke' on an active user. Confirm in the dialog.
testCases.expectedResult: User status changes to 'revoked' in the table. Revoke button disappears for that user.

testCases.title: Create a new test round
testCases.instructions: On the Testing page, click the 'New Round' button in the round selector dropdown. Enter a name like 'Sprint 1 QA' and click Create.
testCases.expectedResult: Round is created and automatically selected. Round name appears as the page header. Status is 'active'.

testCases.title: Switch between test rounds
testCases.instructions: Create 2+ rounds. Open the round selector dropdown and click a different round.
testCases.expectedResult: Round selector shows all rounds. Clicking a round switches the active round and reloads its results.

testCases.title: Edit round name via page header
testCases.instructions: With an active round, click the round name in the page header. Change it to a new name and press Enter.
testCases.expectedResult: Name updates inline. Refreshing the page shows the new name persisted.

testCases.title: Complete a round via button
testCases.instructions: With an active round, click the 'Complete Round' button. Confirm in the dialog.
testCases.expectedResult: Round status changes to 'completed'. Complete Round button disappears. Test action buttons are disabled. Round name is no longer editable.

testCases.title: Round creation shows your name as creator
testCases.instructions: Create a new round and check the round details in the History page.
testCases.expectedResult: Your logged-in name appears as the round creator, not a hardcoded value.

testCases.title: Edit round name with keyboard shortcuts
testCases.instructions: Click the round name header text on the Testing page. Change the text, then test: press Enter to save, press Escape to revert, or click away (blur) to save.
testCases.expectedResult: Enter saves the new name. Escape reverts to the original name without saving. Clicking away (blur) saves the name.

testCases.title: Edit round description
testCases.instructions: Click the 'Click to add description...' text below the round name. Type a description and press Enter.
testCases.expectedResult: Description is saved and displayed. Editing again allows changing or clearing it.

testCases.title: Submit a passing test result
testCases.instructions: With an active round, find a test card and click the green 'Pass' button.
testCases.expectedResult: Card border turns green. Status badge shows 'pass'. Action buttons are replaced with an 'Undo' button.

testCases.title: Submit a failing result with severity
testCases.instructions: Click the red 'Fail' button on a test card. In the Failure Dialog, select 'blocker' severity, enter a description, check 'Create GitHub issue', and click 'Report Failure'.
testCases.expectedResult: Card border turns red. Status badge shows 'fail (blocker)'. If issue was created, issue number link appears on the card.

testCases.title: Submit skip and blocked results
testCases.instructions: Click 'Skip' on one test card and 'Blocked' on another.
testCases.expectedResult: Skip card has yellow border and 'skip' badge. Blocked card has orange border and 'blocked' badge.

testCases.title: Undo a test result
testCases.instructions: After submitting a result, click the 'Undo' button on the test card.
testCases.expectedResult: Card reverts to untested state. Action buttons (Pass/Fail/Skip/Blocked) reappear.

testCases.title: Progress bar updates with results
testCases.instructions: Submit several results with different statuses. Watch the progress bar at the top.
testCases.expectedResult: Progress bar updates in real time showing pass (green), fail (red), skip (yellow), blocked (orange) segments.

testCases.title: Widget script loads from server
testCases.instructions: Visit http://localhost:4747/widget.js in browser.
testCases.expectedResult: JavaScript file loads with PunchlistWidget global. No errors in console.

testCases.title: Widget FAB opens and closes
testCases.instructions: On a page with the widget, click the FAB (bottom-right). Then click the X or click outside.
testCases.expectedResult: Form overlay opens on click, closes on X. FAB reappears when form closes.

testCases.title: Submit support ticket via widget
testCases.instructions: Open widget, fill in subject='Test Bug', select category, add description, submit.
testCases.expectedResult: Success message shown with GitHub issue link. Issue created in repo with correct labels.

testCases.title: Widget captures browser context
testCases.instructions: Submit a support ticket, then check the created GitHub issue body.
testCases.expectedResult: Issue body includes userAgent, pageUrl, screenSize, viewport, timestamp, timezone.

testCases.title: Widget uses shadow DOM for style isolation
testCases.instructions: Inspect the widget element in DevTools. Check for shadowRoot.
testCases.expectedResult: Widget is inside a shadow DOM. Parent page styles do not affect widget appearance.

testCases.title: Widget handles submission failure gracefully
testCases.instructions: Stop the server, try submitting a support ticket.
testCases.expectedResult: Error message shown: 'Something went wrong'. Form values preserved. Try Again button works.

testCases.title: GET /api/config returns project config
testCases.instructions: Authenticated, GET /api/config.
testCases.expectedResult: 200 response with projectName, testCases array, and categories array.

testCases.title: GET /api/commit returns latest SHA
testCases.instructions: Authenticated, GET /api/commit.
testCases.expectedResult: 200 response with sha field containing a git commit hash string.

testCases.title: GET /api/users/me returns safe user info
testCases.instructions: Authenticated, GET /api/users/me. Inspect the response JSON.
testCases.expectedResult: Response contains id, email, name, role. Does NOT contain tokenHash, invitedBy, or revoked.

testCases.title: POST /api/issues creates GitHub issue for test failure
testCases.instructions: POST /api/issues with full createQAFailureOpts payload (testId, testTitle, category, severity, description, testerName, testerEmail).
testCases.expectedResult: 201 response with issue url and number. GitHub issue created with punchlist + qa:fail labels.

testCases.title: GET /api/issues/open/:testId checks for existing issues
testCases.instructions: Create a QA failure issue for auth-001, then GET /api/issues/open/auth-001.
testCases.expectedResult: Returns the open issue with url, number, and title. Returns null data for test IDs with no issues.

testCases.title: Validation errors return structured 400 response
testCases.instructions: POST /api/rounds with empty body {}.
testCases.expectedResult: 400 response with {success: false, error: 'Validation error', details: [{path, message}]}.

testCases.title: Init creates config in current directory
testCases.instructions: In a new directory with a git repo, run 'pnpm cli init'. Follow the prompts.
testCases.expectedResult: punchlist.config.json and .env created. Config has correct repo, storage, and auth sections.

testCases.title: Serve starts with all adapters
testCases.instructions: Ensure .env has both tokens. Run 'pnpm cli serve'.
testCases.expectedResult: Server starts on 4747. Logs show project name, port, CORS domains, dashboard URL, widget URL, API URL.

testCases.title: Invite creates user with token
testCases.instructions: Run 'pnpm cli invite --email test@demo.com --name "Demo Tester"'.
testCases.expectedResult: Invite token printed. User visible in 'pnpm cli users' output.

testCases.title: Revoke disables user access
testCases.instructions: Invite a user, then run 'pnpm cli revoke --email test@demo.com'.
testCases.expectedResult: User status changes to revoked. Login with that token fails with 403.

testCases.title: Users command lists all testers
testCases.instructions: After creating multiple users, run 'pnpm cli users'.
testCases.expectedResult: Table showing all users with email, name, role, and active/revoked status.
